w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: iDaVIE is a VR application for exploring 3D astronomical data cubes. The Feature System lets researchers mark regions of interest within a volume using SoFiA mask files, imported catalogues, or interactive VR drawing. The current design has some structural problems: it mixes Unity types into the domain, packs too many concerns into a single manager, and fetches statistics externally at export time. This document sets out the requirements for a refactor that breaks FeatureSetManager into three focused components: FeatureCatalog, FeatureSetService, and FeatureVisualiser.
Generated from SoFiA mask file via native source-finding statistics
Statistics owned by domain object after construction; no external native struct access
Loaded from VOTable or FITS catalogue
Raw column data retained as key-value pairs on the feature object
Created interactively in VR as a selection box; promotable to a named feature
Lifecycle (create, edit, promote, discard) must not depend on any Unity rendering component

All three flavours implement a common IFeature interface that exposes id, name, flag, centre, bounding-box corners, size, visibility, selection state, and raw data. Code outside the domain should never need to type-check or cast a feature object (NFR-6 LSP). Only one feature can be selected at a time (FR-9).

Statistics are stored directly on the MaskedFeature object when it is constructed, following the GRASP Information Expert principle. Once the factory call completes, nothing outside the domain should hold a reference to the native interop struct (IR-1).
Must lie within the feature bounding box

The IVoTableExporter interface acts as a plug-in seam: it takes a collection of features and a file path, and writes out each feature’s id, centre coordinates, bounding-box corners, sky coordinates (RA, Dec), spectral coordinate, flag, and any imported catalogue columns. The empty export stub in the legacy manager will be replaced by a call through IVoTableExporter routed via FeatureCatalog. No renderer or native struct should be touched during export. Column structure must stay backward compatible (C2).
Persistence and identity management; owns VOTable export via IVoTableExporter
Use-case orchestration: create selection feature, promote box, change selection, trigger export, compute moment maps
Unity-side adapter only; translates domain events into scene-graph operations (geometry, anchor colliders, GPU overlay)

Sub-team 4 (Interaction) calls into FeatureSetService to handle VR-triggered creation and selection (IR-3). The factory constructor agreed with Sub-team 2 (Data I/O) is the only place where native statistics can cross into the domain (IR-1). There is also a deprecated Unity 5 graphics API call in FeatureVisualiser that needs to be updated to the Unity 6 equivalent (IR-4).
Domain classes (Feature, FeatureCatalog, FeatureSetService) have zero transitive Unity dependency
MaskedFeature statistics computable in a unit test with no Unity scene
VOTable export logic reusable with any IEnumerable of features regardless of creation path
Cyclomatic complexity <= 10; cognitive complexity <= 15; max 40 LOC per method; XML docs on all public API boundaries
Adding a new feature flavour touches <= 3 files outside the feature namespace; CBO <= 14 for FeatureCatalog and FeatureSetService
All feature types substitutable behind IFeature; no runtime type-checks outside the domain
Statistics owned and computed by MaskedFeature; not retrieved externally at export or render time
The following CK metric targets apply to all six classes in this refactor (MaskedFeature, ImportedFeature, UserDefinedFeature, FeatureCatalog, FeatureSetService, and FeatureVisualiser):

We’re targeting branch/line coverage of at least 70% for domain code and at least 50% overall. FeatureVisualiser is excluded from the strict targets. All domain and service tests should run without a Unity runtime.
voxel count and flux non-negative; centroid within bounding box; W20 <= W50.

load mask, inspect statistics, edit bounding box via FeatureSetService, then export a VOTable via IVoTableExporter. For unit tests, IFeatureExporter is replaced by a test double; the scenario test uses an in-memory implementation.
Native statistics struct field layout cannot be changed without coordinating with native plugin owners.
VOTable column names and XML structure must remain backward compatible with downstream research tools.
Sprint deliverables are design documents, ADRs, and worked code examples; full implementation is not required.
